title: Corn on the Cob
tagline: Summer in a bite. Three ways: boiled, grilled, or roasted.
description: Perfect corn on the cob every time. Tender, sweet, and slathered with butter.
ingredients: Optional: chili powder, lime, parmesan, fresh herbs
steps: BOILED: Bring a large pot of water to boil. Don't add salt—it toughens the corn. Add corn and cook 4-6 minutes until tender. Drain.
GRILLED: Preheat grill to medium-high. Brush corn with oil. Grill 10-12 minutes, turning every few minutes, until charred in spots.
ROASTED: Preheat oven to 400°F. Place corn directly on oven rack. Roast 20-25 minutes until tender.
Serve immediately with butter, salt, and pepper.
notes: Freshness: Fresh corn is best eaten within 1-2 days. The sugar converts to starch over time.
No salt in water: Salt toughens corn. Season after cooking.
Test for doneness: Pierce a kernel with your fingernail—it should be tender and release milky juice.
Mexican street corn (elote): Coat grilled corn with mayo, cotija cheese, chili powder, and lime.
Cut off the cob: Stand ear upright in a bowl and slice down with a sharp knife.
